reasoning: The 5-year breakeven inflation rate has recently been tracking around 2.32%, closely aligned with the long-term Fed inflation target (which translates to roughly 2.3% on the CPI measure). Because this value is extremely close to the structural mean, future movements are approximately a random walk with a very slight mean-reversion bias towards 2.30%. The short-term linear trend from the past month in FRED data slightly leans downward, producing a model estimate of 0.48 for an increase in the near term. As the forecast horizon increases, the random walk nature dominates, pushing the probabilities closer to an even 0.50. Therefore, I assign probabilities that start slightly below 0.50 for the closest resolution date, and converge cleanly to nominal maximum uncertainty (0.50) for the longer horizons.
